translation_notes: Preserve secrecy marks, sender/recipient lines, and attachment boundaries.
Preserve speaker/question-answer structure. Do not merge separate interrogatory turns.
Prioritize speaker turns. Avoid cutting inside long speeches unless necessary.
Preserve addressee, salutation, signature, and emotional tone.
Preserve numbered claims and rhetorical structure.
Preserve memo headers and any attached materials.
Preserve chronology and first-person narrative structure.
Preserve confrontation exchanges and speaker attribution.
Do not break inside individual list items where possible.
Preserve legal structure and charge numbering.
Preserve operative legal language and sentence terms.
Prefer whole-document translation unless over hard limit.
Keep questions attached to corresponding answers.
Do not merge distinct letters inside correspondence.
Preserve extract status and omitted-text markers.
Preserve line/stanza structure; translation quality may require manual review.
Preserve structured records; avoid prose normalization.
